channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Polarity-tunable field-free room-temperature spin orbit torque switching via topological symmetry breaking in an all-vdW heterostructure for spin logic applications
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 12 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70590-1

Topological interfacial coupling enhances PMA in 2D Fe4GeTe2, yielding coexisting IMA/PMA components enabling field-free SOT switching at low current density with polarity control, demonstrating reconfigurable logic for low-power spintronics.
item.dc:title: Polarity-tunable field-free room-temperature spin orbit torque switching via topological symmetry breaking in an all-vdW heterostructure for spin logic applications
item.dc:creator: Fan Gao
Zili Wang
Runyu Zhao
Jing Li
Xiaoyue Song
Peiyuan Yu
Yun Sun

Guodong Wei

Zongxia Guo
Nicolas Jaouen
Weisheng Zhao
Tianxiao Nie

item.title: Scalable and stretchable 1D multifunctional fibers for multimodal sensing and stimulation
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 11 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70178-9

Fabrication of single liquid-metal fibers is challenged by their limited surface area, thus restricting its functional deployment in soft electronic systems. Here, the authors develop a scalable, solution-deposition strategy for stretchable, multifunctional 1D liquid-metal-based bioelectronic fibers.
item.dc:title: Scalable and stretchable 1D multifunctional fibers for multimodal sensing and stimulation
item.dc:creator: Junyi Yin
Jinjin Zhu
Shaolei Wang
Jiangnan Yuan
Chenyang Li
Samuel Margolis
Hong Bao
Yanan Wang
Wei Zhao
Enbo Zhu
Longlong Mu
Shuai He
Kaidong Wang
Jie Zhao
YongAn Huang
Yunlei Zhou

item.title: Low-frequency ionic-electronic coupling for energy-efficient noise-resilient wireless bioelectronics
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 11 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70331-4

Conventional wireless sensors typically rely on dielectric capacitors with inherently low capacitance, requiring operation at MHz frequencies. Here, the authors develop a wireless low-frequency electrochemical sensing platform that couples ionic dynamics with low-frequency inductor-capacitor resonant circuits.
item.dc:title: Low-frequency ionic-electronic coupling for energy-efficient noise-resilient wireless bioelectronics
item.dc:creator: Ji Hong Kim
Haerim Kim
Jaewon Rhee
Joo Sung Kim
Hanbin Choi
Won Hyuk Choi
Yoseph Park
Jong Hwi Kim
So Young Kim
Seungyoung Ahn
Do Hwan Kim

item.title: Self-regulation of Lewis acid sites on FeOCl toward piezo-self-Fenton reaction for continuous hydroxyl radicals generation
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 11 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70327-0

Designing efficient piezocatalysts requires understanding how mechanical stress alters surfaces. This study shows stress-induced electric potentials boost surface acidity, activate oxygen, and enable hydrogen-peroxide-free pollutant degradation.
item.dc:title: Self-regulation of Lewis acid sites on FeOCl toward piezo-self-Fenton reaction for continuous hydroxyl radicals generation
item.dc:creator: Haojie Dong
Yuanyi Zhou
Zhi Li
Wei Liu
Shaobin Li
Mingshan Zhu
